sanskrit:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः।
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम्॥
transliteration:
jïänena tu tad ajïänaà yeñäà näçitam ätmanaù |
teñäm äditya-vaj jïänaà prakäçayati tat param ||
literal_meaning: By knowledge, that ignorance of those whose self is destroyed, their knowledge, like the rising sun, illuminates that supreme.
contextual_meaning: When the ego’s false narratives are dissolved through self-knowledge, the psyche naturally perceives reality without distortion, just as sunrise ends night-time projections.
psychological_state: Post-insight integration: the mind has dropped defensive distortions and experiences unobstructed perception of self and world.
trigger_scenario: After months of therapy, a client suddenly sees how their ‘I must be perfect’ story created anxiety; the realization feels like the lights coming on in a dark room.
anchor_text: Let self-knowledge dawn and dissolve the ego’s shadow.
